steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
The angles of any triangle sum to 180°:
$$\angle D + \angle E + \angle F = 180°$$
Substituting the given value $\angle D = 114°$:
$$114° + \angle E + \angle F = 180°$$
$$\angle E + \angle F = 66°$$

steps.title: Determine which choice satisfies the constraint
steps.content:
For a valid triangle, both $\angle E$ and $\angle F$ must be positive angles. Since $\angle E + \angle F = 66°$ and both must be positive, we need $0° < \angle E < 66°$.

Testing each choice:
  • A) $115°$ — exceeds $66°$, so $\angle F$ would be negative
  • B) $90°$ — exceeds $66°$, so $\angle F$ would be negative
  • C) $67°$ — exceeds $66°$, so $\angle F$ would be negative
  • D) $65°$ — less than $66°$, so $\angle F = 1°$ (valid)

That matches choice D — D is correct.
